advocacyHeadline: 🚲🌳 Studets for Fremont: SPUSD students deserve streets where we can walk, bike, and roll to school without fear. Tell leaders why safe routes and active transportation should be a priority.

statements.title: We need safer streets so we can get to school on our own
statements.body:
Dear Councilmembers,

I am a middle/high school student in South Pasadena. Many of us want to walk or bike to school, but fast traffic and poor quality bike lanes make that feel unsafe. Fremont can be so much nicer if we slow cars, shorten crossings, and give people on foot and on bike safe routes to school.

When streets are safer, more of us can get to school on our own — and our families worry less.

statements.title: Safe routes to school should work for everyone, not just drivers
statements.body:
Dear Councilmembers,

I am writing as a student. My friends and I should be able to get to school by walking, biking, or scooting without having to compete with rush-hour cut-through traffic. The redesign of Fremont Avenue should make it work for everyone, not just drivers. Routes near schools need good crosswalks and protection at intersections.

statements.title: Students for Fremont:
statements.body:
Dear Councilmembers,

As a SPUSD student, I constantly hear about the need to act to stop climate change. The city has an opportunity to do that by redesigning Fremont Avenue to be safer for walking and biking. When more of us can walk, bike, or roll to school safely, we reduce the car emissions that cause climate change and bad air quality.

 Students will use new sidewalks and bike lanes — and our community's carbon footprint will go down.
